content.text: Connections
The EP-133 features comprehensive connectivity on its top panel including audio I/O, MIDI, sync, and USB. All ports use 3.5mm or USB-C connectors for compact design.
Audio Output
The OUTPUT port is a 3.5mm stereo jack providing the main audio output. Connect to headphones, powered speakers, a mixer, or an audio interface.
Audio Input
The INPUT port (orange) accepts line-level audio for sampling. Connect phones, tablets, turntables (with preamp), synthesizers, or other audio sources.
Speaker
The built-in mono speaker provides monitoring without headphones. It's automatically disabled when headphones or line output is connected.
USB
The USB-C port provides multiple functions: power, USB MIDI, firmware updates, and sample transfer. It's class-compliant for plug-and-play operation.

items: Power: Runs the EP-133 or charges alongside batteries
USB MIDI: Send and receive MIDI without additional interfaces
Firmware: Update to the latest OS version
Sample Transfer: Import/export samples via the EP-133 app

content.text: Power
The EP-133 runs on 4x AAA batteries or USB power. Battery life is approximately 8 hours. The power switch is a physical slider on the top panel.

content.title: Power Options
content.text: Use USB power for extended sessions. Batteries provide backup and portability. The EP-133 can run on USB while batteries are installed for hot-swap capability.
Connection Examples
Headphone Setup
Connect headphones to the OUTPUT port. Use the VOLUME knob to adjust listening level. This is the simplest setup for portable use.
Recording to a Computer
Connect OUTPUT to your audio interface's input. Connect USB for MIDI sync and control. Your DAW can record EP-133 audio while sending MIDI clock.
Live Performance
Connect OUTPUT to a mixer or PA system. Use SYNC or MIDI to sync with other performers' gear. The built-in speaker is disabled when output is connected.
Sampling from Vinyl
Connect a turntable's preamp output (or a turntable with built-in preamp) to INPUT. Adjust GAIN for proper level, and sample directly from records.
